title: Leucanthemum latifolium
intro: Leucanthemum latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved daisy, is a perennial flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum latifolium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum latifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy family. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which includes approximately 40 species of daisies. Leucanthemum latifolium is further classified as a perennial plant, native to Europe and Western Asia. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics, growth habits, and evolutionary relationships with other species. Its classification also assists in the identification and differentiation of Leucanthemum latifolium from other closely related species.
uses: Leucanthemum latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved oxeye daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for its various properties. Although its use is not widely documented, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to be used in herbal remedies to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Additionally, the plant is used in some cultures to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Leucanthemum latifolium as a medicinal plant.

preparations: Leucanthemum latifolium, commonly known as the broad-leaved oxeye daisy, has been used in herbal preparations to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of bloating.
